rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope; do not introduce new architecture.
When diagnostics report type mismatches or '<error>' types, trace and resolve the root cause of the first unresolved identifier on the same line.
Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type scopes; move valid bindings to implementation annexes or remove invalid clauses.
Ensure AGREE expressions preserve type consistency: numeric variables must receive numeric expressions, Boolean variables must receive Boolean expressions.
generic_rules: Generated AGREE code must only reference identifiers visible in the current scope.
AGREE contracts should use only declared constants, equations, or existing component features.
Type consistency is required for all AGREE expressions; avoid mixing incompatible types like int and <error>.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers unless they are explicitly imported or defined in the model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use `assign output = expression;` only for valid output features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation scope.
Constants and equations in AGREE must have explicit types: int, real, or bool.
Guarantees should be placed in component type scopes unless they reference implementation-level features.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to resolve missing identifiers by inventing new declarations or architecture elements.
Do not mix incompatible types in binary expressions such as int = <error> or <error> = int.
Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes unless they reference visible ports.
Do not assume AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) are automatically available as AGREE variables.
